free_text: 17O+58Ni scattering and reaction dynamics around the
Coulomb barrier
Beam intensity is 15 nA.
150 ug/cm2 thick 58Ni
50 ug/cm2 thick 208Pb (backing)
17O+208Pb elastic scattering is used for cross section
normalization assuming that it is pure Rutherford
scattering.
300 um thick Si strip detector at 49.4 and
-49.5 deg (covering 36 to 65 deg)
43 um and 300 um Si strip detectors
at -110.8 deg (covering 94 to 125 deg).
Approved by Emanuele Strano (2016-11-04)
M.Odsuren and N.Otsuka
E.Strano (2017-01-11): We found both theoretically
and experimentally that the excitation of 17O is
negligible. In this case the scattered 17O can be
considered in its ground state.
Error due to the uncertainty in the target
thickness and the statistical error due to the
process discrimination procedure were added to the
statistical error on the average cross section.
Plotted in Fig.8 of Phys.Rev.C94(2016)024622
Received by e-mail from E.Strano
E.Strano (2017-01-11): After the 1n stripping 16O is
surely in its ground state since the excitation to
the first level requires 6 MeV (resulting in a well
separated peak that was not observed) whereas 59Ni
may be in several excited states, which were
considered in the theoretical calculation and
tabulated in Table I. Experimentally we only
observed the cumulative effect.
The error due to the uncertainty in the
target thickness and the statistical error due to the
process discrimination procedure were added to the
statistical error on the average cross section.
Plotted in Fig.9 of Phys.Rev.C94(2016)024622
Received by e-mail from E.Strano
Statistical error, 2% error due to
uncertainty in the ratio between the thicknesses of
the Pb and Ni layers, and the uncertainty related to
the process discrimination procedure.
Plotted in Fig.10 of Phys.Rev.C94(2016)024622
Received by e-mail from E.Strano
